title: [ImportVerilog] Fix crash on enum traversal methods
body:
The `first`/`last`/`next`/`prev`/`num` system call names are shared between associative array methods and enum built-in methods. The code previously assumed these were always associative array methods and asserted on argument count and type, causing an abort when enum methods like `val.next()` were encountered.

Guard the associative array handling with an `isAssociativeArray()` check on the first argument, and fall through to emit an "unsupported system call" diagnostic for enum and queue uses of these methods.
